A study done among California homeowners shows the power of loss framing quite clearly. The homeowners received an energy audit from a local power company official who told them how to put insulation and weather stripping into their homes to conserve energy. Half were told, if you fully insulate your home, you will be able to save a dollar a day every day. The other half were told, if you fail to insulate fully, you will lose a dollar a day every day. Only more people insulated their homes under loss than gain language. The same thing, a dollar, when couched in terms of loss, resonated more forcefully. That's because when making a decision, people are more interested in information about what they stand to lose than what they stand to gain. Why wouldn't we give them that information? It's the information they want.
